behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on what the real LLM generates for signature structure. A stubbed model can force a correct signature and hide real mismatch failures. This is a single model-output-quality check, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run a live generation for prompts with strict expected signatures, parse the returned code signature, and assert exact match on parameter names/types and return type.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is module handoff/orchestration logic (attempt loop, test runner result consumption, early exit). It does not require real OS/network/subprocess behavior, and correctness does not depend on LLM semantic quality if attempt outcomes are stubbed deterministically.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator + attempt store + stubbed generator + stubbed test executor; force fail/pass sequence and assert ordered execution and immediate stop on first pass.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a live iterative loop with refinement across attempts. Correctness depends on multiple real LLM decisions in sequence (initial generation plus subsequent refinement), so single-step agentic coverage is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known prompt where first attempt often fails; run live generate->test->refine->retest loop and assert pass occurs within <=3 attempts.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: The real failure boundary is data handoff between test-result collection and prompt construction. This can be caught with deterministic local wiring; no real model judgment is needed because assertion is on prompt content itself.
behaviors.test_description: Feed realistic failed-test outputs through test-result parser + prompt builder path and assert next prompt contains each failed test name and failure message.

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: This requires sequential live model behavior: prior failure context must influence a later generation. A mock could always emit different code or fixed code and miss whether the real loop actually adapts to context. Multiple agentic steps are intrinsic.
behaviors.test_description: Run live attempt 1 to failure, capture failure context sent to refinement, run attempt 2 live, and assert attempt-2 code materially differs and addresses the specific failed assertion area.

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic orchestration + bookkeeping + error-shaping across local modules. Real infrastructure and real LLM output quality are not required if failures are stubbed.
behaviors.test_description: With generator/test executor stubs that force three failures, run the loop and assert termination at attempt 3 plus structured error payload containing all attempts and per-attempt test results.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text explicitly scopes to non-agentic deterministic stubs. Target failures are wiring/state propagation bugs across modules, which are caught in-process without real OS/network/model variability.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end pipeline with deterministic generator stubs and deterministic test outcomes; assert correct call sequence, state transitions, and final status.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a full live multi-step loop with at least two model-dependent stages and success criterion tied to end-to-end adaptive behavior. Lower levels or single-step agentic tests cannot validate this chained behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use a benchmark hard function known to fail first pass; execute live loop end to end and assert first attempt fails, subsequent refinement attempts occur, and one later attempt passes.
